title: Longest Palindromic Substring with K Swaps is NOT Unique

statement.markdown: Given a string s and an integer k, return the length of the longest palindromic substring that can be obtained by at most k swaps.
statement.inputFormat: Input is a string and an integer
statement.outputFormat: Output is the length of the longest palindromic substring
statement.notes: At most k swaps are allowed

hints.text: Use a dynamic programming approach to build up a table of longest palindromic substrings
Use the table to find the longest palindromic substring with at most k swaps

editorial.title: Solving Longest Palindromic Substring with K Swaps
editorial.intuition: The problem can be solved using dynamic programming.
editorial.bruteForce: The brute force approach would be to try all possible substrings and check if they are palindromes. However, this approach is too slow.
editorial.betterSolution: We can use a dynamic programming approach to build up a table of longest palindromic substrings.
editorial.optimalSolution: We can use the table to find the longest palindromic substring with at most k swaps.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The proof of correctness is that we are correctly calculating the longest palindromic substring with at most k swaps.
editorial.dryRun: An example of a dry run would be to try input s = "abcba" and k = 0.
commonMistakes: Not correctly calculating the longest palindromic substring

testCases.input: First line string length, second line string elements
